original_description: Generate professional, on-brand PDFs from markdown using docsmith's design-system templates. Use this whenever the user wants to turn markdown / notes / a report / an outline into a polished PDF, make a "handbook" or "slide deck", render to the BGN (Badan Gizi Nasional) or Claude/"claudecode" brand, produce a "professional PDF / good-looking PDF", or render one source to several templates at once. Handles inline ```d2 diagrams (rendered once, shared across templates). Templates today: `handbook` (LaTeX book via pandoc+tectonic), `bgn-deck` and `claudecode-deck` (16:9 slides via marp-cli). Do NOT use for editing existing PDFs or for plain `pandoc file.md` with no styling need.
best_description: Generate professional, on-brand PDFs from markdown using docsmith's design-system templates. Use this whenever the user wants to turn markdown / notes / a report / an outline into a polished PDF, make a "handbook" or "slide deck", render to the BGN (Badan Gizi Nasional) or Claude/"claudecode" brand, produce a "professional PDF / good-looking PDF", or render one source to several templates at once. Handles inline ```d2 diagrams (rendered once, shared across templates). Templates today: `handbook` (LaTeX book via pandoc+tectonic), `bgn-deck` and `claudecode-deck` (16:9 slides via marp-cli). Do NOT use for editing existing PDFs or for plain `pandoc file.md` with no styling need.
final_description: Build a finished PDF document out of markdown. Use this for ANY request to make, build, export, generate, render, or "turn into" a PDF from a .md file, notes, an outline, or a report draft — this is the markdown-to-PDF document builder. Reach for it especially when the output is a presentation slide deck (16:9, "claudecode-style", a branded corporate deck), a handbook or PDF book (chapters, cover, tip/warning callout boxes), or anything wanted polished, professional, good-looking, on-brand, in "our design system", or in a named brand like BGN / Badan Gizi Nasional or Claude/claudecode. Also use it when the markdown embeds diagrams (e.g. d2) that must appear in the PDF, or when one source should yield several layouts at once. Prefer it over hand-running pandoc or marp. Do NOT use it on PDFs that already exist (editing, merging, compressing, translating, extracting text).

history.description: Generate professional, on-brand PDFs from markdown using docsmith's design-system templates. Use this whenever the user wants to turn markdown / notes / a report / an outline into a polished PDF, make a "handbook" or "slide deck", render to the BGN (Badan Gizi Nasional) or Claude/"claudecode" brand, produce a "professional PDF / good-looking PDF", or render one source to several templates at once. Handles inline ```d2 diagrams (rendered once, shared across templates). Templates today: `handbook` (LaTeX book via pandoc+tectonic), `bgn-deck` and `claudecode-deck` (16:9 slides via marp-cli). Do NOT use for editing existing PDFs or for plain `pandoc file.md` with no styling need.

history.description: Use this skill to turn a markdown file into a finished, designed PDF — whenever the user wants something that looks styled, branded, or presentation-ready rather than a plain export. Triggers include: "make a slide deck / slides / 16:9 deck PDF", "build a handbook or PDF book with chapters and a cover", "produce a polished / good-looking / professional / on-brand PDF", "render this in our design system / brand", or naming a brand like BGN (Badan Gizi Nasional) or Claude/claudecode. Also fires when the source markdown includes diagrams (e.g. d2) that should appear in the PDF, or when one source should render to several layouts at once. Convert markdown / notes / outlines / report drafts into these outputs. Do NOT use for editing, merging, compressing, translating, or extracting text from existing PDFs, or for a plain unstyled markdown-to-PDF conversion with no design intent.

history.description: Use this skill whenever the user wants to turn markdown into a PDF — converting a .md file, notes, an outline, or a report draft into a finished document. Use it for every markdown-to-PDF need: slide decks (slides, "16:9 deck", "claudecode-style deck"), handbooks or PDF books (chapters, covers, tip/warning callout boxes), and styled or branded output ("polished", "professional", "good-looking", "on-brand", "our design system", or a named brand like BGN / Badan Gizi Nasional or Claude/claudecode). Also use it when the markdown contains diagrams (e.g. d2) that should render into the PDF, or when one source should produce several layouts at once. Prefer this skill over running pandoc manually. Do NOT use it to edit, merge, compress, translate, or extract text from PDFs that already exist.

history.description: When someone has markdown (a .md file, notes, an outline, a report draft) and wants a real document out of it, use this skill to produce the PDF. It is the right tool for any "make / build / export / generate this as a PDF" request, and especially for designed output: presentation slide decks (16:9, claudecode-style), handbooks and PDF books (chapters, covers, tip/warning callout boxes), and anything described as polished, professional, good-looking, on-brand, "our design system", or a named brand such as BGN / Badan Gizi Nasional or Claude/claudecode. Also use it when the markdown embeds diagrams (e.g. d2) that must render into the PDF, or when one source should yield several layouts at once. Always choose this over a hand-run pandoc or marp command. Skip it only for operations on PDFs that already exist — editing, merging, compressing, translating, or extracting text.
